purpose: DX-WWW launch proof for converting large modern web products into Forge-owned routes.

routes.state: real source conversion, partial and blocked runtime

real: Routes, manifests, receipts, copied assets, and license notices exist.
Source files were selected from local mirrors and recorded by path and commit.
Visual route audits record section, responsive, accessibility, and asset proof for every converted route.
Route discovery now gives DX-WWW, DX CLI, and Zed one canonical source-owned index for /ui, /database, and /backend.
Studio preview selectors map route DOM markers back to converted pages, surface maps, visual audits, manifests, and receipts.
A no-runtime route acceptance checklist records the evidence required before rendered-proof promotion.
A rendered-proof receipt schema gives future runtime verification a stable output contract under .dx/forge/runtime-evidence.
A source-only rendered-proof validator reports missing runtime evidence without failing source readiness.
A blocked rendered-proof sample receipt records the not-approved fixture shape without fake runtime artifacts.
A rendered-proof import plan and dry-run reporter map approved external receipts to their final runtime-evidence targets.
A rendered-proof completeness reviewer explains why the proof remains 99/100 until real runtime receipts exist.
A rendered-proof runtime approval request packet records the exact runtime capture scope before any server, build, credentialed backend, or evidence write is allowed.
A rendered-proof evidence authoring guide tells runtime lanes what to collect without creating fake screenshots, placeholder hashes, or source-lane receipts.
partial: Interactive runtime behavior is represented structurally.
No renderer screenshot was taken because local servers were out of scope.
blocked: No npm build, cargo check, cargo test, or long local server was allowed.
Supabase and Convex live backends require explicit credentials and runtime integration.
